features.name: Jack of Trades
features.description: Starting 2nd level, a Bard can add their proficiency bonus to any ability check they make that doesn't already include a proficiency bonus.

features.name: Bardic Inspiration
features.description: By 10th level, a Bard can use their Bardic Inspiration feature up to three times per day. Each time, until the end of the current turn, they can grant advantage on an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw made by a creature within earshot (including themselves). The granted bonus equals their Charisma modifier.

features.name: Bardic Inspiration (D8)
features.description: At 15th level, the Bard's daily uses of Bardic Inspiration increase to four.

features.name: Superior Inspiration
features.description: Starting at 20th level, a Bard can use their Bardic Inspiration feature as a bonus action on their turn (instead of waiting until another creature takes an action). Additionally, if they do not have any uses of Bardic Inspiration remaining, they can regain one use by completing a short or long rest.

features.name: Font of Inspiration
features.description: At 5th level, the Bard regains their expended Bardic Inspiration uses when they finish a short or long rest.

features.name: Expertise
features.description: At 3rd level, two of the Bard's proficiencies are doubled. At 10th level, the Bard can gain another proficiency in this way.

features.name: Song of Rest
features.description: Starting at 2nd level, a Bard uses soothing music or an oration to help allies revitalize during a short rest. Any creature that hears the performance regains hit points equal to twice the Bard's Charisma modifier (a minimum of 1). If any of those creatures are wounded at the end of the short rest, they regain an extra ld6 hit points.

features.name: Bard College
features.description: Beginning at 3rd level, a Bard selects one of six colleges of magic to grant them access to additional features and spells. These include Archivist, College of Lore, College of Monks, College of Glamour, College of Valor, and College of Whispers.

features.name: Additional Colleges
features.description: At 14th level, the Bard can select a second college of magic from those available. If they choose to swap out their first college for another, the previous college's features are lost.

features.name: Countercharm
features.description: Early in this class (before leveling up), a Bard learns how to counteract charm effects, using it voluntarily as an action or ending an effect early without requiring an action.

features.name: Magical Secrets
features.description: Starting at 14th level, a Bard can cast spells from the spellbook of another class. They must have already prepared and be able to cast that spell using their own spellcasting ability before doing so.
